title: Preah Khan Temple - World Monuments Fund

highlights: name "Preah Khan" means “Sacred Sword of Victory,” derived from the ancient capital Jayashri (“Holy City of  ...    1. The First Enclosure (Central Sanctuary)This enclosure is the core of the sacred heart of the temple. It consists of the central tower (once housing the principal deity image), surrounded by smaller sanctuaries and corridors. There are four entrances aligned with the cardinal points, linked to processional paths. It represents Mount Meru, the cosmic axis in Hindu-Buddhist cosmology. The central sanctuary was dedicated to Buddhism, while the northern and western shrines were devoted to Brahmanism, Vishnu (west), and Shiva (north).
